member.name: John Slinger
member.synopsis: John Slinger is the Labour MP for Rugby , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to support businesses reliant on imported steel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Based on 2025 data, three-quarters of UK steel imports by value (74%) and over half by volume (53%) are outside scope of steel trade measure.Following extensive engagement with UK industry, the final quota volume increased by 21% compared to our provision...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Music: Teachers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what support her Department gives to qualified peripatetic and school music teachers to help them deliver music lessons.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department provides £76 million per annum for the Music Hub network that employs or contracts with over 9,000 peripatetic teaching staff across England. Music Hubs work with classroom teachers in over 90% of schools.The Music Hub network provides cont...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Craniocervical Instability: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of a clear NHS pathway for diagnosing and managing craniocervical instability; what measures are available to protect patients; and how patient
writtenQuestions.answerText: Craniocervical instability (CCI) is a complex presentation that can arise in the context of a range of underlying conditions. At present, there is no agreed national diagnostic definition or evidence base to support a distinct National Health Service path...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to financially support community pharmacies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For 2025/26, funding for the core community pharmacy contractual framework was increased to £3.1 billion. This represented the largest uplift in funding of any part of the National Health Service at the time, at over 19% across 2024/25 and 2025/26.On 25 F...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Brain: Tumours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of automatically utilising brain tumour patients’ anonymised data to create relevant datasets for clinical research.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department invests over £1.6 billion per year in research through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). Cancer is a major area of NIHR spending at £141.6 million in 2024/25, reflecting its high priority.We are committed to furthe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what role the Betting and Gaming Council has had in informing Government policy on gambling advertising; if she will publish the Betting and Gaming Council’s report on gambling advertising; and w
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to consulting the best available evidence on gambling advertising from a wide range of sources when assessing next policy steps in this space. It is for the Betting and Gaming Council to decide whether to publish their report o...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has considered allowing dental therapists to expand their scope of practice and hold their own NHS contracts; and what assessment his Department has made of the potential imp
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 10-Year Health Plan for England and our recent Quality and Payment Reforms consultation outline the Government’s commitment to utilising the skills of the wider dental team to improve access to dental care. Amendments to the Human Medicines Regulation...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his department has considered replacing the routine six-monthly dental check-up with a system based on individual clinical need.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Patients should be recalled based on their clinical need. The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) guidance on recall intervals states that a healthy adult with good oral health should see a dentist once every two years, and a child on...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to the press release entitled Study reveals how conversational AI can exert influence over political beliefs, published by the University of Oxford on 11 December, what a
writtenQuestions.answerText: The trajectory of AI development is uncertain and evolving quickly. New AI technologies, including ‘chatbot’ style applications built on frontier AI models, offer both opportunities and risks. It is imperative that we to understand these impacts from a sc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Belfast Agreement: European Convention on Human Rights
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of the UK's membership of the European Convention on Human Rights on the Good Friday Agreement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is a proud signatory to the European Convention on Human Rights which underpins many international agreements on trade, security and migration, as well as the Good Friday Agreement. That Agreement also required domestic incorporation of the ECHR in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Northern Ireland Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Media: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to help improve media literacy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government aims to improve media literacy through cross-government collaboration, educational initiatives and collaboration with Ofcom, civil society, and online platforms. This approach supports Ofcom's regulatory media literacy duties and promotes m...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Mobile Phones
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what evidence her Department has on the potential impact of banning smartphone use in schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Mobile phones have no place in school and school leaders already have the power to ban them. The department’s guidance on mobile phones in schools, published in February 2024, is clear that schools should prohibit the use of devices with smart technology ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Men
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to raise awareness among men and boys about the mental health support available to them.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The 10-Year Health Plan sets out ambitious plans to transform mental health services to improve access and treatment, and to promote good mental health and wellbeing for men and boys. The Government is committed to working beyond the health system to crea...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Iraq: Kurds
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of the relationship between the Federal Government of Iraq and the Kurdistan Region of Iraq.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our diplomatic presence, we regularly engage with the Government of Iraq and the Kurdistan Regional Government. We continue to encourage constructive dialogue and cooperation between Baghdad and Erbil to resolve their outstanding issues, including...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Iraq: Kurds
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what representations he has made to his Iraqi counterpart on the suspension of budget transfers to the Kurdistan Region of Iraq.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Although the Foreign Secretary has not made any direct representations to his Iraqi counterpart on the suspension of budget transfers to the Kurdistan Region of Iraq (KRI), the UK Government continues to encourage through our regular diplomatic engagement...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Licensing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether her Department plans to review the Gambling Commission’s remit to include UK licensees operating in prohibited markets abroad.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Operators providing gambling facilities to customers in Great Britain must be licensed by the Gambling Commission and comply with the conditions of their operating licences. The Commission expects them to obey the laws of all other jurisdictions in which ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will take steps to introduce prostate cancer testing for men at earlier ages than is currently available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Screening for prostate cancer is currently not recommended by the UK National Screening Committees (UK NSC). This is because of the inaccuracy of the current best test, called the Prostate Specific Antigen (PSA). A PSA-based screening programme could harm...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Further Education: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: What steps her Department is taking to help further education colleges recruit vocational teachers from industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises that industry experience is vital in the high quality teaching of vocational and technical education.The national further education teacher recruitment campaign, “Share Your Skills”, targets those with industry skills to think ab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Kurds: Elections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of the outcome of the recent parliamentary elections in the Kurdistan region in Iraq.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK was pleased to welcome the Kurdistan Region of Iraq's parliamentary election which took place on 20 October 2024. This election was an essential opportunity for the people of the Region to have their voices heard, as demonstrated by the high turnou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Motorway Service Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her plans for regulation of the private parking industry will apply to motorway service station car parks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s Private Parking Code of Practice will outline specific requirements for the operation and management of all private parking facilities, including motorway service area car parks.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

items.text: Name of donor: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Address of donor: C/o Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia, 30 Charles Street, London W1J 5DZ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights from London to Riyadh (£7,276.54), hotel accommodation (£1,880.31) and food (£300), value £9,456.85 Destination of visit: Saudi Arabia (Riyadh) Dates of visit: 11 October 2025 to 15 October 2025 Purpose of visit: To further the aims of the APPG on Saudi Arabia, to strengthen bilateral relations, promote cultural exchange, and address key issues such as trade and regional stability via discussions, visits, and events to deepen mutual cooperation. (Registered 31 October 2025)

Borough councillor (Rugby). On my request Rugby Council stoped paying my allowance from 4 July 2024 (when I was elected) onwards. The allowance up to this date did not meet the threshold to be registered. I resigned as a councillor on 25 March 2025, triggering a By-election which took place on 1 May 2025. Rugby Borough Council have stated my official resignation date is 5 May 2025 (three days after the election). Date interest ended: 5 May 2025 (Registered 30 August 2024; updated 12 June 2025)
Member of the Executive Committee of the British Group of the Inter-Parliamentary Union. Date interest arose: 13 November 2024 (Registered 27 February 2025)
